title: Not Opening Your Bills Costs You $24,000
hook: Forty-two percent of Americans won't open their financial statements tonight — and that one habit of looking away has a $24,000 price tag.
angle: Financial avoidance isn't just laziness — it's a documented psychological coping mechanism that compounds silently into late fees, missed fraud, uncaught billing errors, and IRS penalties over a decade
audience: Anyone who has ever put off opening a bill, checking their bank statement, or filing taxes — which is most people
key_numbers: $247 average fraudulent charge missed in 60-day window
$40 per late fee x 3 times per year
5% per month IRS late filing penalty up to 25%
